# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-deployment
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: your-registry/your-webapp:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          name: http
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "200m"
            memory: "256Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: http
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: memcached-deployment
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: memcached
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: memcached
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: memcached
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: memcached
        image: memcached:1.6.9
        ports:
        - containerPort: 11211
          name: memcached
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "100m"
            memory: "128Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "200m"
            memory: "256Mi"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: memcached-service
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: memcached
spec:
  selector:
    app: memcached
  ports:
  - protocol: TCP
    port: 11211
    targetPort: memcached
    name: memcached
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: worker-deployment
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: worker
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: worker
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: worker
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: worker
        image: your-registry/your-worker:v1.0.0
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "150m"
            memory: "256Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "300m"
            memory: "512Mi"
        # Example of a liveness probe for a worker that might expose a health endpoint
        # or simply check if the process is running. Adjust as per your worker's design.
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - cat
            - /tmp/healthy
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - cat
            - /tmp/ready
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
